title: quercus pauciradiata
intro: Quercus pauciradiata, also known as the few-flowered oak, is a species of deciduous tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. The leaves of Quercus pauciradiata contain flavonoids and tannins, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing fever, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Quercus pauciradiata can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can be used in infusions or dried for later use in herbal remedies.
